checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the revised menu approach against `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,774-803`. Folding the final-video actions into the existing `shotGroupMenu` state is aligned with the current shot-group UI pattern and does resolve the earlier duplicate-menu concern.

Checked the revised dismissal model against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts:160-166` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`. The plan still dismisses by `shotId`, not by final-video generation id, so if a shot gets a newer final output later in the same session the badge would remain suppressed even though `useShotFinalVideos()` would surface a different `id`; that earlier concern is still open.

Checked the updated single-clip scope against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:29-33,73-75` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts:101-110`. The revised plan now explicitly answers that question by keeping the existing `>= 2` clip threshold, which matches how shot groups are actually defined in the repository.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked the direct asset-registration revision against `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:148-156` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:238-257`. Creating an `AssetRegistryEntry` with `type: 'video/mp4'` and calling `patchRegistry()` plus `registerAsset()` is technically compatible with the repo and does resolve the prior `registerGenerationAsset()`/`mimeType` contract problem.

Checked the no-duration-fetch decision against `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35`, and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`. The repository still derives clip length from `to - from`, and the final-video query still provides no duration metadata, so the revised plan still cannot prove that `to: endTime - startTime` is safe for shorter source media; a short final video can still be asked to play longer than its actual content.

Checked the hand-built replacement clip against the existing insert path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:201-252,262-271` and serialization in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:210-216`. The revised `TimelineEditor` sketch still bypasses current insertion invariants such as manual-track geometry defaults and explicit `clipOrder` updates, so replacement clips on manual visual tracks will not be initialized the same way as other inserted video clips and may serialize in a different per-track order.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Checked related asset-insertion code in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:150-271` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:24-33`. The broader repository pattern for adding timeline media is centralized there, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, selection updates, and `clipOrder` maintenance, so the revised plan is still broader than a pure menu/UI change and needs either shared helper extraction or a more explicit statement that those invariants are being replicated on purpose.

Checked cross-tool boundaries in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:14` and the final-video hook in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-62`. Importing `useShotFinalVideos()` into the video editor still fits the repo’s current boundaries because `TimelineEditor` already depends on travel-between-images code via `VideoGenerationModal`.

Checked the menu-state revision against `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,757-803`. Reusing the existing `shotGroupMenu` state is the right containment boundary for this feature and does avoid the parallel-menu sprawl that the previous version would have introduced.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: Checked the revised verification plan against the existing component tests in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx:133-260` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts:1-114`. The plan now explicitly skips wiring tests, so badge rendering, badge clickability, menu item presence, and group-payload propagation remain untested even though this repository already has test files in the exact components being changed.

Checked the history expectations against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts:198-220`. The success criteria still require the switch action to behave as a single undo step, but the revised plan still relies only on manual smoke testing for undo behavior, so the most important user-facing history guarantee remains unverified.

Checked the existing mutation-test pattern in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts:47-123`. The repo already has a lightweight way to assert exact `applyEdit()` payloads, so a focused test for the final-video replacement mutation would be straightforward; choosing TypeScript compilation alone leaves the new direct `patchRegistry/registerAsset` path and the atomic rows edit without any automated regression coverage.

flags.concern: Final-video reminder state: dismissing by `shotId` instead of final-video id still suppresses later final outputs for the same shot during the same session.
flags.evidence: The revised plan keeps `dismissedShotIds: Set<string>` in `useFinalVideoAvailable`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` surfaces per-shot final-video records keyed by a changing generation `id` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`.

flags.concern: Timeline media duration: the revised plan still sizes the final-video clip to the shot-group span without any duration source, so shorter final videos can still overrun their real media length.
flags.evidence: `useShotFinalVideos()` fetches no duration in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:23-27`, while timeline duration is derived from `clip.to - clip.from` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35` and passed directly to the Remotion `Video` trim props in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`.

flags.concern: Video-editor asset insertion: the revised plan still hand-rolls media insertion in `TimelineEditor.tsx` instead of reusing or extracting the existing asset-management path, so manual-track defaults and clip-order invariants remain easy to miss.
flags.evidence: Current insert behavior in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:201-252,262-271` includes manual-track geometry defaults and `updateClipOrder()`, but the revised replacement sketch only creates a bare `videoMeta` and calls `applyEdit()` without a `clipOrderOverride`.

flags.concern: Timeline canvas/menu verification: the revised plan still skips tests for badge rendering, clickability, and menu wiring even though those are the main changed surfaces.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts` already exist, but the revised plan limits validation to `tsc`, `useShotGroups.test.ts`, and manual smoke testing.

flags.concern: Undo-history verification: the revised plan still treats single-step undo as a must-have outcome without any automated check that executes the action and undoes it.
flags.evidence: The success criteria require a single atomic replace and manual smoke mentions undo, but no automated test is proposed against the history behavior implemented in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts:198-220`.
